entities.name: FOOD INSECURITY
entities.description: A condition characterized by the lack of consistent access to enough food for an active, healthy life, persisting in many parts of Africa and Asia

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A location where food insecurity persists and where agricultural research for development interventions are implemented

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A location where food insecurity persists and where agricultural research for development interventions are implemented

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH FOR DEVELOPMENT (AR4D)
entities.description: A conceptual framework for research interventions aimed at improving agricultural outcomes to address food insecurity

entities.name: THEORY OF CHANGE (TOC)
entities.description: A method that enables stakeholders to present and test theories and assumptions about how and why impact occurs within complex systems

entities.name: LITERATURE REVIEW
entities.description: A research method used to identify challenges and opportunities in the mainstreaming of Theory of Change

entities.name: INTERVIEWS
entities.description: A research method involving 26 experts in African and Asian food security to triangulate findings on Theory of Change

entities.name: LOGFRAMES
entities.description: A project management method often used in parallel with or confused with Theory of Change

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: A location where a case study of the evolution of a Theory of Change in an AR4D project was conducted over four years

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: A concept representing the long-term goal of ensuring all people have access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: A concept referring to the overall improvement of agricultural productivity and systems to support food security

relationships.description: approach for designing and evaluating interventions in
used to study challenges of mainstreaming
